rationale: The page contains detailed regulatory information regarding the National Sports Governance (National Sports Bodies) Rules, 2026, and the National Sports Board and Tribunal. It includes specific institutional structures, eligibility criteria, and legal jurisdictions, which are high-value for governance-related questions.
cleanedText:
National Sports Governance Framework (Continued):
* There shall be only one National Olympic Committee and one National Paralympic Committee, each governing multi-sport disciplines.
National Sports Board (NSB):
* Central Government establishes NSB, consisting of a Chairperson and such number of other Members as may be prescribed.
* Central government will decide its composition and appoint members on recommendations of search-cum-selection committee.
* Powers and functions:
* Grant recognition to any sports organisation as NSB and register their affiliate unit.
* Only recognised bodies will receive funds from central government.
* Issue guidelines for formulation of Code of Ethics by National Sports Bodies.
* Constitute an ad hoc administrative body or to direct the concerned National Sports Body or their affiliate units.
* Frame Safe Sports Policy for protection and safety of women, minor athletes and other classes of persons.
* A recognised organisation shall be considered a public authority under RTI Act, 2005.
National Sports Tribunal:
* To adjudicate “sports-related disputes”.
* Composition: Chairperson and two other members.
* Chairperson shall be a person who is or has been, a Judge of Supreme Court or Chief Justice of a High Court.
* Two members: Secretary to Government of India in Ministry of Law and Justice and in Department of Sports.
* Vested with power of civil court.
* Exclusion of jurisdiction of Tribunal:
* Any dispute, or conflict arising during Olympic Games, Paralympic Games, Commonwealth Games, Asian Games.
* Any dispute falling within exclusive jurisdiction of internal disputes resolution committee of a National Sports Body; or any other tribunal or court; or any International Federation; or Court of Arbitration for Sports established in Lausanne, Switzerland.
* Doping related disputes.
Oversight of elections:
* Central government will establish a national panel of electoral officers to oversee elections of national sports bodies.
* Every national sports body must constitute an electoral panel to oversee elections of its affiliates.
7.4.1. NATIONAL SPORTS GOVERNANCE (NATIONAL SPORTS BODIES) RULES, 2026
Why in the news?
Recently, Union Government has notified the National Sports Governance (National Sports Bodies) Rules, 2026 under National Sports Governance Act, 2025.
About National Sports Governance (National Sports Bodies) Rules, 2026
* Each National Sports Body to have a General body and an executive committee.
* Composition of General Body: At least 4 sportspersons of outstanding merit (SOMs), with 50% being women.
* Eligibility of SOMs: Not less than 25 years of age and must have retired from active sports and should have not participated in any competitive sport event which leads to selection to represent a district, State or India for at least one year prior to the date of application.
* Women’s representation in Executive Committee: specific posts to be reserved for women to ensure at least 4 women members.
